title: Coefficient
short: The numerical multiplier in front of a variable term, like the 3 in 3x².
full:
A coefficient is the number multiplying a variable part of a term: in 3x² the coefficient is 3, in −5x it is −5. The constant term (with no variable) can be thought of as the coefficient of x⁰ = 1.

Coefficients are what you read off when matching or comparing polynomials, and they determine the roots of an equation.

They are ordinary numbers, so they carry no special units.
